ops.title: Codex async subagent wake WORKS via the cc-interact steering plane (v6, shipped 1.0.1)
ops.body:
Supersedes "wake is a harness gap, foreground-block is convergent-correct" (2660990b). The gap analysis stands — an in-process subagent still has no NATIVE interrupt inlet (#78782 + 8 siblings + #76203, evidence consolidated in issuecomment-5029376501) — but the conclusion changed: cc-interact's steering plane (landed post-v0.12.0, verified live on CC 2.1.214) delivers an external wake through a four-rung ladder, and codex plugin 1.0.0/1.0.1 ships it end to end as one nested Go binary at cc-skills plugins/codex (module github.com/yasyf/cc-skills/plugins/codex).

Shape: codex-ask is both the dispatch/registry CLI (byte-compatible Go port of the Python, 33 goldens) and a cc-interact consumer (per-user daemon at ~/.cc-codex-ask, daemonrole-installed). An owner subagent runs `codex-ask --dispatch --owner <id>` (dispatch-time Session/Scope/ClaudePID capture into the lane's cmd file), parks on the channel's `await` MCP tool, and the detached worker wakes it on any terminal state through directWake (OpAgentDirect; bounded 30s, fail-open, wake-only — the directive names status + reply_file, never the payload; disk is truth). Park is primary; the parent SendMessage relay (greeting-primed) is the fallback; `--await` disk recovery backstops a dead daemon. Top-level sessions use Monitor + `codex-ask --watch` (proven live: settle record pinged the chat with a real reply on disk). Foreground blocking stays the default; the guard hook still blocks backgrounding and now routes to the sanctioned async paths.

Proven live 2026-07-20: matrix items 1 (instant dispatch + registry), 2 (Monitor + --watch chat ping), 6 (daemon lazy-autostart at the daemonrole path); wake enqueue+drain hermetically (TestOwnerWakeDirectiveLands, real daemon + real worker + directive observed; pid-fallback under session rotation). Items 3-5 (in-harness parked-owner wake, relay, park cancel) are cc-notes task 7e1146ad — needs a fresh session (channel mounts at session start). Release: v1.0.1 tag → release-go.yml@v1 → signed cask → brew 1.0.1 → pinned install-binary.sh symlink provisioning (v1.0.1 fixed AGENTS.md resolution through the symlink — the live matrix caught it; HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN added to cc-skills from 1Password after the first release run failed the cask push).
